name: Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga
description: One of the twelve sacred Jyotirlingas, this ancient temple on the banks of Shipra River is famous for its unique Bhasma Aarti and its south-facing idol.
history: The temple is mentioned in the Puranas. The current structure was rebuilt by the Scindia dynasty in the 18th century after being destroyed by Iltutmish in 1235.
famousFor: Bhasma Aarti, Jyotirlinga, and Kumbh Mela.
